The first pass found no existing `effect` package imports in backend code, which suggests this may be more of an adoption than a straight API migration. I’m reading the backend entry points and package metadata next to verify the actual runtime and dependency shape.

{
  "name": "relay",
  "version": "0.1.0",
  "description": "Local-first Codex-driven kanban desktop app.",
  "main": "./out/main/index.js",
  "type": "module",
  "private": true,
  "scripts": {
    "dev": "electron-vite dev",
    "dev:logs": "electron-vite dev 2>&1 | tee /tmp/relay-dev.log",
    "build": "tsc --noEmit && electron-vite build",
    "logs": "mkdir -p \"$HOME/Library/Application Support/relay\" && touch \"$HOME/Library/Application Support/relay/relay.log\" && tail -n 200 -f \"$HOME/Library/Application Support/relay/relay.log\"",
    "logs:dev": "touch /tmp/relay-dev.log && tail -n 200 -f /tmp/relay-dev.log",
    "preview": "electron-vite preview",
    "test": "node tests/run-tests.mjs",
    "typecheck": "tsc --noEmit"
  },
  "dependencies": {

The repo has no existing Effect dependency or migration notes, and the backend is currently plain Promise/async code around Electron, storage, Git, and Codex SDK. The package target remains ambiguous, so I’m checking whether npm metadata is reachable; if it is not, I’ll avoid guessing a version blindly.
